article.title: Uber | L3 | Bangalore | Interview Experience
article.content:
**About me:**
College: Tier 2
Education: BTech( IT )
Years of Experience: 1 year 8 months
Previous company: MNC Bank
Prev Comp: 18 LPA

Role: SDE 1 (L3)
Comapany: Uber
Status: Cleared & Offer Accepted

**Round 1: Online Assessment**
4 leetcode Questions. Solved 3 completely and 1 partially.


**Round 2: Technical Interview 1 (DSA + Debugging)**
Duration: 60 mins

Question: Given a log file with rider information (name, city_visited, timestamp), make unique triplets of cities visited by the order of the timestamp and return the number of riders for each triplet that have visted those cities in the exact same order.

Follow-up: 
    a. if the information is too large for it to be stored in memory.
    b. if you want to solve this faster.

Solved optimally. The interviewer was happy with the answer.

Tip: Think aloud. Discuss solution before coding. Ask as many clarifying questions as needed.

**Round 3: Design (L3-focused)**
Duration: 60 mins

Ouestion: Given an employee management system, write the code for
    a. adding an employee under a manager
    b. giving the total count under a manager

Started with different approach but eventually realised the fault in my thinking pattern and had enough time to start over after explaining my thoughts to interviewer.


**Round 4: Behavioral + Project Deep Dive (EM Round)**
Questions: 
    Discussions around my work experience and projects.
    STAR questions: team conflict, conflicted opinion with manager over a decision, fast delivery under pressure, why uber

**Verdict: Cleared/ Offer Accepted**

After 4 days, recruiter informed me that it's a hire call and we can proceed with the team matching.
After 10-15 days had a call with hiring manager.
took 20 days for offer rollout from thr day of verdict.

Compensation: https://leetcode.com/discuss/post/6696559/uber-l3-bengaluru-accepted-by-anonymous_-pcyk/
GS interview Ex: https://leetcode.com/discuss/post/6725538/goldman-sachs-analyst-gbm-interview-expe-0wh0/

post.content: Compensation link redirects to this experience. i guess we are in recursion without a stop condition.
Uber asked me a question to rearrange the tickets. He expected DFS solution. I provided the topological sort solution. Interviewer was amazed and I had to explain how it worked.\n\nRest is history.
Did you apply for sde-2 and got a call for sde-1 ??

> Ouestion: Given an employee management system, write the code for
a. adding an employee under a manager
b. giving the total count under a manager

What is the expected time complexity here?
Bro how did you get the call?
How did you get this opportunity? Did recruiter reached out to you or did you apply through the referral?
